350â†’		StepResults: []StepResult{},
   351â†’	}
   352â†’
   353â†’	// Step 1: Clone repository
   354â†’	repoPath := filepath.Join(r.workspaceDir, "repo")
   355â†’	if err := r.gitOps.CloneRepository(ctx, r.config.TargetRepo, r.config.BaseRef, repoPath); err != nil {
   356â†’		result.ErrorMessage = fmt.Sprintf("failed to clone repository: %v", err)
   357â†’		result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   358â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to clone repository: %w", err)
   359â†’	}
   360â†’	result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   361â†’		StepID:  "clone",
   362â†’		Success: true,
   363â†’		Message: fmt.Sprintf("Cloned %s at %s", r.config.TargetRepo, r.config.BaseRef),
   364â†’	})
   365â†’
   366â†’	// Step 2: Create and checkout workflow branch
   367â†’	branchName := GenerateBranchName(r.config.ID)
   368â†’	result.BranchName = branchName
   369â†’	if err := r.gitOps.CreateBranchAndCheckout(ctx, repoPath, branchName); err != nil {
   370â†’		result.ErrorMessage = fmt.Sprintf("failed to create branch: %v", err)
   371â†’		result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   372â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create branch: %w", err)
   373â†’	}
   374â†’	result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   375â†’		StepID:  "create-branch",
   376â†’		Success: true,
   377â†’		Message: fmt.Sprintf("Created workflow branch: %s", branchName),
   378â†’	})
   379â†’
   380â†’	// Step 3: Execute recipe steps
   381â†’	for _, step := range r.config.Steps {
   382â†’		if step.Type == "recipe" {
   383â†’			stepStart := time.Now()
   384â†’			if err := r.recipeExecutor.ExecuteRecipes(ctx, repoPath, step.Recipes); err != nil {
   385â†’				result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   386â†’					StepID:   step.ID,
   387â†’					Success:  false,
   388â†’					Message:  fmt.Sprintf("Recipe execution failed: %v", err),
   389â†’					Duration: time.Since(stepStart),
   390â†’				})
   391â†’				result.ErrorMessage = fmt.Sprintf("failed to execute recipes: %v", err)
   392â†’				result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   393â†’				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to execute recipes: %w", err)
   394â†’			}
   395â†’
   396â†’			result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   397â†’				StepID:   step.ID,
   398â†’				Success:  true,
   399â†’				Message:  fmt.Sprintf("Applied recipe: %s", strings.Join(step.Recipes, ", ")),
   400â†’				Duration: time.Since(stepStart),
   401â†’			})
   402â†’		}
   403â†’	}
   404â†’
   405â†’	// Step 4: Commit changes
   406â†’	commitMessage := fmt.Sprintf("Applied recipe transformations for workflow %s", r.config.ID)
   407â†’	if err := r.gitOps.CommitChanges(ctx, repoPath, commitMessage); err != nil {
   408â†’		result.ErrorMessage = fmt.Sprintf("failed to commit changes: %v", err)
   409â†’		result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   410â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to commit changes: %w", err)
   411â†’	}
   412â†’	result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   413â†’		StepID:  "commit",
   414â†’		Success: true,
   415â†’		Message: "Committed changes",
   416â†’	})
   417â†’
   418â†’	// Step 5: Run build check
   419â†’	buildStart := time.Now()
   420â†’	appName := GenerateAppName(r.config.ID)
   421â†’	timeout, err := r.config.ParseBuildTimeout()
   422â†’	if err != nil {
   423â†’		result.ErrorMessage = fmt.Sprintf("invalid build timeout: %v", err)
   424â†’		result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   425â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid build timeout: %w", err)
   426â†’	}
   427â†’
   428â†’	buildConfig := common.DeployConfig{
   429â†’		App:           appName,
   430â†’		Lane:          r.config.Lane,
   431â†’		Environment:   "dev",
   432â†’		Timeout:       timeout,
   433â†’		ControllerURL: "", // Will be set by the actual implementation
   434â†’	}
   435â†’
   436â†’	buildResult, err := r.buildChecker.CheckBuild(ctx, buildConfig)
   437â†’	if err != nil || (buildResult != nil && !buildResult.Success) {
   438â†’		message := "Build check failed"
   439â†’		if buildResult != nil && buildResult.Message != "" {
   440â†’			message = buildResult.Message
   441â†’		}
   442â†’		if err != nil {
   443â†’			message = fmt.Sprintf("%s: %v", message, err)
   444â†’		}
   445â†’
   446â†’		result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   447â†’			StepID:   "build",
   448â†’			Success:  false,
   449â†’			Message:  message,
   450â†’			Duration: time.Since(buildStart),
   451â†’		})
   452â†’
   453â†’		// Check if self-healing is enabled
   454â†’		if r.config.SelfHeal != nil && r.config.SelfHeal.Enabled && r.jobSubmitter != nil {
   455â†’			// Attempt healing workflow
   456â†’			healingSummary, healingErr := r.attemptHealing(ctx, repoPath, message)
   457â†’			result.HealingSummary = healingSummary
   458â†’
   459â†’			if healingErr == nil && healingSummary.Winner != nil {
   460â†’				// Healing succeeded! Continue with the healed version
   461â†’				result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   462â†’					StepID:   "healing",
   463â†’					Success:  true,
   464â†’					Message:  fmt.Sprintf("Healing succeeded with plan %s", healingSummary.PlanID),
   465â†’					Duration: time.Since(buildStart),
   466â†’				})
   467â†’				// Continue with normal workflow (push, etc.)
   468â†’			} else {
   469â†’				// Healing also failed
   470â†’				result.ErrorMessage = "build check failed and healing failed"
   471â†’				result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   472â†’				return nil, fmt.Errorf("build check failed: %s (healing also failed: %v)", message, healingErr)
   473â†’			}
   474â†’		} else {
   475â†’			// No healing enabled, fail immediately
   476â†’			result.ErrorMessage = "build check failed"
   477â†’			result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   478â†’			return nil, fmt.Errorf("build check failed: %s", message)
   479â†’		}
   480â†’	}
   481â†’
   482â†’	if buildResult != nil {
   483â†’		result.BuildVersion = buildResult.Version
   484â†’	}
   485â†’	result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   486â†’		StepID:   "build",
   487â†’		Success:  true,
   488â†’		Message:  "Build completed successfully",
   489â†’		Duration: time.Since(buildStart),
   490â†’	})
   491â†’
   492â†’	// Step 6: Push branch
   493â†’	if err := r.gitOps.PushBranch(ctx, repoPath, r.config.TargetRepo, branchName); err != nil {
   494â†’		result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   495â†’			StepID:  "push",
   496â†’			Success: false,
   497â†’			Message: fmt.Sprintf("Push failed: %v", err),
   498â†’		})
   499â†’		result.ErrorMessage = fmt.Sprintf("failed to push branch: %v", err)
   500â†’		result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   501â†’		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to push branch: %w", err)
   502â†’	}
   503â†’	result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   504â†’		StepID:  "push",
   505â†’		Success: true,
   506â†’		Message: fmt.Sprintf("Pushed branch %s", branchName),
   507â†’	})
   508â†’
   509â†’	// Step 7: Create or update GitLab merge request (if GitProvider is configured)
   510â†’	if r.gitProvider != nil {
   511â†’		mrStart := time.Now()
   512â†’		if err := r.gitProvider.ValidateConfiguration(); err != nil {
   513â†’			// MR creation is optional - log but don't fail the workflow
   514â†’			result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   515â†’				StepID:   "mr",
   516â†’				Success:  false,
   517â†’				Message:  fmt.Sprintf("MR creation skipped - configuration invalid: %v", err),
   518â†’				Duration: time.Since(mrStart),
   519â†’			})
   520â†’		} else {
   521â†’			mrConfig := provider.MRConfig{
   522â†’				RepoURL:      r.config.TargetRepo,
   523â†’				SourceBranch: branchName,
   524â†’				TargetBranch: r.config.BaseRef,
   525â†’				Title:        fmt.Sprintf("Transflow: %s", r.config.ID),
   526â†’				Description:  r.generateMRDescription(result),
   527â†’				Labels:       []string{"ploy", "tfl"},
   528â†’			}
   529â†’
   530â†’			mrResult, err := r.gitProvider.CreateOrUpdateMR(ctx, mrConfig)
   531â†’			if err != nil {
   532â†’				// MR creation is optional - log but don't fail the workflow
   533â†’				result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   534â†’					StepID:   "mr",
   535â†’					Success:  false,
   536â†’					Message:  fmt.Sprintf("MR creation failed: %v", err),
   537â†’					Duration: time.Since(mrStart),
   538â†’				})
   539â†’			} else {
   540â†’				action := "created"
   541â†’				if !mrResult.Created {
   542â†’					action = "updated"
   543â†’				}
   544â†’				result.StepResults = append(result.StepResults, StepResult{
   545â†’					StepID:   "mr",
   546â†’					Success:  true,
   547â†’					Message:  fmt.Sprintf("MR %s: %s", action, mrResult.MRURL),
   548â†’					Duration: time.Since(mrStart),
   549â†’				})
   550â†’				result.MRURL = mrResult.MRURL
   551â†’			}
   552â†’		}
   553â†’	}
   554â†’
   555â†’	// Success!
   556â†’	result.Success = true
   557â†’	result.Duration = time.Since(startTime)
   558â†’	return result, nil
   559â†’}
   560â†’
   561â†’// generateMRDescription creates a descriptive merge request body based on workflow results
   562â†’func (r *TransflowRunner) generateMRDescription(result *TransflowResult) string {
   563â†’	var description strings.Builder
   564â†’
   565â†’	description.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("## Transflow Workflow: %s\n\n", r.config.ID))
   566â†’
   567â†’	// Add basic workflow information
   568â†’	description.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("**Branch:** %s\n", result.BranchName))
   569â†’	if result.BuildVersion != "" {
   570â†’		description.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("**Build Version:** %s\n", result.BuildVersion))
   571â†’	}
   572â†’	description.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("**Duration:** %s\n\n", result.Duration.String()))
   573â†’
   574â†’	// Add applied transformations
   575â†’	description.WriteString("## Applied Transformations\n\n")
   576â†’	for _, step := range result.StepResults {
   577â†’		if step.StepID != "mr" && step.Success {
   578â†’			description.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("- âś… **%s**: %s\n", strings.Title(step.StepID), step.Message))
   579â†’		}
   580â†’	}
   581â†’
   582â†’	// Add healing information if present
   583â†’	if result.HealingSummary != nil && result.HealingSummary.Winner != nil {
   584â†’		description.WriteString("\n## Self-Healing Applied\n\n")
   585â†’		description.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("- **Plan ID:** %s\n", result.HealingSummary.PlanID))
   586â†’		description.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("- **Winning Strategy:** %s\n", result.HealingSummary.Winner.ID))
   587â†’		description.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("- **Attempts:** %d\n", result.HealingSummary.AttemptsCount))
   588â†’	}
   589â†’
   590â†’	// Add footer with automation info
   591â†’	description.WriteString("\n---\n")
   592â†’	description.WriteString("đź¤– *This merge request was automatically created by Ploy Transflow*\n")
   593â†’	description.WriteString("đź“ť *Labels: ploy, tfl*")
   594â†’
   595â†’	return description.String()
   596â†’}
   597â†’
   598â†’// attemptHealing orchestrates the healing workflow: planner â†’ fanout â†’ reducer
   599â†’func (r *TransflowRunner) attemptHealing(ctx context.Context, repoPath string, buildError string) (*TransflowHealingSummary, error) {
   600â†’	summary := &TransflowHealingSummary{
   601â†’		Enabled:       true,
   602â†’		AttemptsCount: 1,
   603â†’	}
   604â†’
   605â†’	// Step 1: Submit planner job to analyze the build error
   606â†’	jobHelper := NewJobSubmissionHelperWithRunner(r.jobSubmitter, r)
   607â†’	planResult, err := jobHelper.SubmitPlannerJob(ctx, r.config, buildError, r.workspaceDir)
   608â†’	if err != nil {
   609â†’		return summary, fmt.Errorf("planner job failed: %w", err)
   610â†’	}
   611â†’
   612â†’	summary.PlanID = planResult.PlanID
   613â†’
   614â†’	// Step 2: Convert planner options to branch specs
   615â†’	var branches []BranchSpec
   616â†’	for i, option := range planResult.Options {
   617â†’		branchID := fmt.Sprintf("option-%d", i)
   618â†’		if id, ok := option["id"].(string); ok {
   619â†’			branchID = id
   620â†’		}
   621â†’
   622â†’		branchType := "llm-exec" // default
   623â†’		if t, ok := option["type"].(string); ok {
   624â†’			branchType = t
   625â†’		}
   626â†’
   627â†’		branches = append(branches, BranchSpec{
   628â†’			ID:     branchID,
   629â†’			Type:   branchType,
   630â†’			Inputs: option,
   631â†’		})
   632â†’	}
   633â†’
   634â†’	// Step 3: Execute fanout orchestration
   635â†’	orchestrator := NewFanoutOrchestratorWithRunner(r.jobSubmitter, r)
   636â†’	maxParallel := 3 // Default parallelism
   637â†’	if r.config.SelfHeal.MaxRetries > 0 {
   638â†’		maxParallel = r.config.SelfHeal.MaxRetries
   639â†’	}
   640â†’
   641â†’	winner, allResults, err := orchestrator.RunHealingFanout(ctx, nil, branches, maxParallel)
   642â†’	summary.AllResults = allResults
   643â†’
   644â†’	if err != nil {
   645â†’		// Fanout failed, but continue to reducer anyway
   646â†’		summary.Winner = nil
   647â†’	} else {
   648â†’		summary.Winner = &winner
   649â†’	}
   650â†’
   651â†’	// Step 4: Submit reducer job to determine next action
   652â†’	nextAction, reducerErr := jobHelper.SubmitReducerJob(ctx, planResult.PlanID, allResults, summary.Winner, r.workspaceDir)
   653â†’	if reducerErr != nil {
   654â†’		return summary, fmt.Errorf("reducer job failed: %w", reducerErr)
   655â†’	}
   656â†’
   657â†’	// If reducer says to stop and we have a winner, healing succeeded
   658â†’	if nextAction.Action == "stop" && summary.Winner != nil {
   659â†’		summary.SetFinalResult(true)
   660â†’		return summary, nil
   661â†’	}
   662â†’
   663â†’	// Otherwise, healing failed
   664â†’	return summary, fmt.Errorf("healing failed: %s", nextAction.Notes)
   665â†’}
   666â†’
   667â†’// CleanupWorkspace removes the temporary workspace directory
   668â†’func (r *TransflowRunner) CleanupWorkspace() error {
   669â†’	if r.workspaceDir != "" {
   670â†’		return os.RemoveAll(r.workspaceDir)
   671â†’	}
   672â†’	return nil
   673â†’}
   674â†’

content.text: Now let me examine the roadmap documentation to understand the current state:

content.content:
1â†’## MVP: transflow (Minimal Viable Implementation)
     2â†’
     3â†’Purpose: deliver a basic, working transflow that can apply OpenRewrite recipes, heal build errors via LangGraph planner/reducer jobs with parallel options, and open a GitLab MR.
     4â†’
     5â†’## Implementation Status
     6â†’
     7â†’âś… **Fully Implemented:**
     8â†’- OpenRewrite recipe execution with ARF integration
     9â†’- Build check via `/v1/apps/:app/builds` (sandbox mode, no deploy)
    10â†’- YAML configuration parsing and validation
    11â†’- Git operations (clone, branch, commit, push)
    12â†’- Diff validation and application utilities
    13â†’- GitLab MR integration with environment variable configuration
    14â†’- Complete CLI integration (`ploy transflow run`) with full end-to-end workflow
    15â†’- Test mode infrastructure with mock implementations for CI/testing
    16â†’
    17â†’âš ď¸Ź **Partially Implemented:**
    18â†’- LangGraph healing (planner/reducer infrastructure exists, orchestration in progress)
    19â†’- Self-healing config parsing and data structures
    20â†’- Job templates for healing branches (human-step, llm-exec, orw-generated)
    21â†’
    22â†’âťŚ **Not Implemented:**
    23â†’- Model registry in `ployman` CLI
    24â†’- KB (knowledge base) read/write for learning
    25â†’
    26â†’### In-Scope (Must Work)
    27â†’
    28â†’- OpenRewrite recipe execution
    29â†’  - Reuse existing ARF/OpenRewrite pipeline (services/openrewrite-jvm, API/CLI `ploy arf transform`).
    30â†’  - Input: repo URL + branch, ordered recipe IDs. Output: local patch/commit applied to a workflow branch.
    31â†’
    32â†’ - LLM plan and exec
    33â†’  - `llm-exec` (Stream 2/Phase 1): run model with prompts + prefetched context (repo paths, HTTPS URLs), apply generated patch, commit; execute as Nomad jobs via internal/orchestration.
    34â†’  - `llm-plan` (Stream 2/Phase 2): produce a list of `llm-exec` steps (sequential only in MVP) from repo state + last error.
    35â†’  - Model registry: minimal CRUD in `ployman` CLI with schema validation; stored under `llms` namespace.
    36â†’  - MCP tools: declare per-step (MCP spec), env-only config; prefetch context per execution (no cache/persist).
    37â†’
    38â†’- Build check (sandbox mode, no deploy)
    39â†’  - Use existing build API to verify the code builds without deploying.
    40â†’  - Client: `internal/cli/common/deploy.go::SharedPush` (POST tarball to controller `POST /v1/apps/:app/builds?sha=...&env=dev[&lane=...]`).
    41â†’  - Server handler: `internal/build/trigger.go` performs lane detection (if lane not provided), builds artifacts (Unikraft/OSv/OCI/etc.), signs, generates SBOM, uploads to storage; it does not deploy to Nomad.
    42â†’  - Success criteria: HTTP 200; response includes `lane`, `image` or `dockerImage`. For logs, optional `GET /v1/apps/:app/logs`.
    43â†’  - Status endpoint (optional): `GET /v1/apps/:app/status` for additional visibility.
    44â†’  - App naming: derive unique app name `tfw-<transflow-id>-<timestamp>` unless explicitly provided.
    45â†’  - Lane override: allow explicit `lane` in transflow; fallback to auto-detect when absent.
    46â†’  - Timeout: request timeout configurable per run; default 10m for build check.
    47â†’
    48â†’ - Git operations + MR (Stream 3)
    49â†’  - Create a workflow branch at start; commit changes from steps; push branch to origin using provided token envs.
    50â†’  - GitLab MR: infer project from `target_repo`, target branch is `base_ref`; envs `GITLAB_URL`, `GITLAB_TOKEN`.
    51â†’  - Apply default MR labels/scope: `ploy`, `tfl` (if available).
    52â†’
    53â†’- LangGraph healing (planner/reducer as jobs)
    54â†’  - Planner job: Input = repo metadata + last_error + KB snapshot; Output = `plan.json` with parallel options: humanâ€‘step, llmâ€‘exec, orwâ€‘generated.
    55â†’  - Orchestrator fanâ€‘out: run each option as an independent Nomad job (branch); first success wins; cancel others.
    56â†’  - Reducer job: Input = branch results + winner; Output = next actions (usually stop; else new `plan.json`).
    57â†’  - KB: cases and summaries persisted in SeaweedFS; locks via Consul KV; error/patch dedup via normalized signatures/fingerprints.
    58â†’  - Consistent learning & dedup: see `roadmap/transflow/kb.md`. Planner reads `summary.json`; branches write `cases/` and patch blobs; compactor maintains summaries and optional vector bundles.
    59â†’  - Job types:
    60â†’    - human-step: wait for human MR/commit; runner polls branch; success if build passes afterward.
    61â†’    - llm-exec: generate diff-only patch (MCP tools allowed); apply and build-check.
    62â†’    - orw-gen â†’ openrewrite: generate ORW recipe (class/coords) via LLM; run OpenRewrite job; build-check.
    63â†’
    64â†’- Transflow runner (orchestrator)
    65â†’  - Parse `roadmap/transflow/transflow.yaml` and run steps sequentially: recipe â†’ llm-plan â†’ llm-exec â†’ MR.
    66â†’  - Minimal logging; write per-step logs and final summary.
    67â†’
    68â†’### What Already Exists (Leverage)
    69â†’
    70â†’- OpenRewrite execution: `services/openrewrite-jvm`, ARF endpoints and CLI (`internal/cli/arf/*`, `api/arf/*`).
    71â†’- Git clone/diff/commit helpers: `api/arf/git_operations.go` (extend with push).
    72â†’- Lane/build/deploy system (not used in MVP flow): lane detection, Nomad templates, push deploy (`internal/cli/common/deploy.go`).
    73â†’- Git provider envs expected for MR (GitLab): set via environment variables.
    74â†’
    75â†’### New Work (Minimal)
    76â†’
    77â†’- New `transflow` orchestrator
    78â†’  - CLI entry: `ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml`.
    79â†’  - Step engine: call existing ARF recipe execution; implement LLM plan/exec runners; manage workflow branch lifecycle; run Build check before MR.
    80â†’
    81â†’- LLM runners
    82â†’  - `llm-exec`: container/job that receives model+prompts+context; produces unified diff or patch; apply and commit (used in parallel healing options).
    83â†’  - MCP injection via env; context prefetcher for repo files and HTTPS URLs.
    84â†’
    85â†’- Model registry
    86â†’  - `ployman models {list|get|add|update|delete}` with basic schema validation; stored alongside existing recipe metadata.
    87â†’
    88â†’ - MR integration (GitLab)
    89â†’  - Push branch using token env; call GitLab REST to create/update MR (project inferred from `target_repo` URL, source branch, target=`base_ref`), adding default labels `ploy`,`tfl` when supported.
    90â†’
    91â†’- Build check integration
    92â†’  - Implement a `build` step that tars the workspace and invokes `SharedPush` with `IsPlatform=false`, `Environment=dev`, and optional lane override from transflow.
    93â†’  - Expand `DeployConfig` to include `Timeout time.Duration`; `SharedPush` honors `config.Timeout` sourced from transflow's global `build_timeout`.
    94â†’  - Treat non-200 as build failure; record response JSON in step logs for diagnostics.
    95â†’
    96â†’ - LangGraph planner/reducer integration
    97â†’  - Add job templates and CLI/orchestrator glue to invoke planner after a failing build, and reducer after branch completion.
    98â†’  - Persist `plan.json`, branch job results, and a compact run manifest; firstâ€‘successâ€‘wins cancellation.
    99â†’
   100â†’### Interfaces (High-Level)
   101â†’
   102â†’- Input: `roadmap/transflow/transflow.yaml` (id, repo, branch, steps; global lane/build_timeout).
   103â†’- Output: GitLab MR link, `plan.json`/branch results, step logs, final summary JSON.
   104â†’- Env: `GITLAB_URL`, `GITLAB_TOKEN` for MR; model/MCP creds via env only.
   105â†’
   106â†’### Out of Scope (MVP)
   107â†’
   108â†’- Parallel execution and branch-per-exec merging.
   109â†’- Secure network policy and allowlists.
   110â†’- Budgets/limits (tokens, cost, time).
   111â†’- Static analysis, vulnerability scans, compile gates.
   112â†’- Deploy/test gates and lane-driven runtime checks.
   113â†’- SeaweedFS/Consul KV persistence beyond what ARF already uses implicitly.
   114â†’- Auto-formatters outside of step-specific behavior.
   115â†’- End-to-end integration tests; coverage thresholds.
   116â†’
   117â†’### Risks and Mitigations
   118â†’
   119â†’- LLM determinism: constrain outputs to unified diff/patch; validate before apply.
   120â†’- Context quality: ensure prefetch resolves globs/URLs deterministically at run time; log sources.
   121â†’- MR creation: handle idempotency (update existing MR for same branch).
   122â†’
   123â†’### Next Steps
   124â†’
   125â†’1) Add `ploy transflow run` CLI + YAML parser. 2) Wire recipe step to ARF. 3) Implement LangGraph planner/reducer jobs and orchestrator fanâ€‘out logic. 4) Implement LLMâ€‘exec runner with MCP/env/context prefetch (for branch options). 5) Implement git push + GitLab MR creation. 6) Minimal logs and summary output.
   126â†’### Test Case: JDK 11 â†’ 17 Migration
   127â†’
   128â†’- Minimal transform request (single OpenRewrite recipe step):
   129â†’
   130â†’POST /v1/transforms
   131â†’Content-Type: application/json
   132â†’
   133â†’{
   134â†’  "id": "java11to17",
   135â†’  "target_repo": "https://git.example.com/org/app.git",
   136â†’  "base_ref": "refs/heads/main",
   137â†’  "target_branch": "refs/heads/main",
   138â†’  "lane": "C",
   139â†’  "steps": [
   140â†’    {
   141â†’      "type": "recipe",
   142â†’      "engine": "openrewrite",
   143â†’      "recipes": [
   144â†’        "org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java11toJava17"
   145â†’      ]
   146â†’    }
   147â†’  ]
   148â†’}
   149â†’
   150â†’- Expected flow:
   151â†’  1) Apply ORW recipe; commit on workflow/<id>/<timestamp>.
   152â†’  2) Build check via /v1/apps/:app/builds (no deploy).
   153â†’  3) If build fails, run LangGraph planner job; branches: human-step, llm-exec, orw-genâ†’openrewrite.
   154â†’  4) First success wins; reducer finalizes next actions (usually stop).
   155â†’
   156â†’## Test Repository
   157â†’
   158â†’**Java 11 Maven Test Repository for OpenRewrite Migration Testing:**
   159â†’- **URL**: https://gitlab.com/iw2rmb/ploy-orw-java11-maven.git
   160â†’- **Purpose**: Real Java 11 codebase with OpenRewrite-transformable patterns for testing complete transflow workflows
   161â†’- **Contains**: Maven project structure, Java 11 code patterns (string operations, Optional usage, stream processing), JUnit 5 tests
   162â†’- **Used by**: Self-healing tests, integration tests, CLI validation with real repository scenarios
   163â†’- **Configuration**: Pre-configured with OpenRewrite Maven plugin for Java 11â†’17 migration recipes
   164â†’
   165â†’## CLI Usage and Examples
   166â†’
   167â†’### Basic Usage
   168â†’
   169â†’```bash
   170â†’# Run a complete transflow workflow
   171â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml
   172â†’
   173â†’# Run with verbose output
   174â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --verbose
   175â†’
   176â†’# Dry run to validate configuration
   177â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --dry-run
   178â†’
   179â†’# Use test mode for development/CI
   180â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --test-mode
   181â†’```
   182â†’
   183â†’### Configuration Examples
   184â†’
   185â†’**Basic Java 11â†’17 Migration:**
   186â†’```yaml
   187â†’# transflow.yaml
   188â†’version: v1alpha1
   189â†’id: java11-to-17-migration
   190â†’target_repo: https://gitlab.com/your-org/your-java-project.git
   191â†’target_branch: refs/heads/main
   192â†’base_ref: refs/heads/main
   193â†’lane: C
   194â†’build_timeout: 15m
   195â†’
   196â†’steps:
   197â†’  - type: recipe
   198â†’    id: java-migration
   199â†’    engine: openrewrite
   200â†’    recipes:
   201â†’      - org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java11toJava17
   202â†’      - org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.CommonStaticAnalysis
   203â†’      - org.openrewrite.java.RemoveUnusedImports
   204â†’
   205â†’self_heal:
   206â†’  enabled: true
   207â†’  max_retries: 2
   208â†’  cooldown: 30s
   209â†’```
   210â†’
   211â†’**With GitLab MR Creation:**
   212â†’```bash
   213â†’# Set GitLab environment variables
   214â†’export GITLAB_URL=https://gitlab.com
   215â†’export GITLAB_TOKEN=your-gitlab-token
   216â†’
   217â†’# Run workflow with MR creation
   218â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml
   219â†’```
   220â†’
   221â†’**Multiple Recipe Steps:**
   222â†’```yaml
   223â†’version: v1alpha1
   224â†’id: comprehensive-cleanup
   225â†’target_repo: https://gitlab.com/your-org/legacy-project.git
   226â†’target_branch: refs/heads/modernization
   227â†’base_ref: refs/heads/main
   228â†’
   229â†’steps:
   230â†’  - type: recipe
   231â†’    id: import-cleanup
   232â†’    engine: openrewrite
   233â†’    recipes:
   234â†’      - org.openrewrite.java.RemoveUnusedImports
   235â†’      - org.openrewrite.java.OrderImports
   236â†’
   237â†’  - type: recipe
   238â†’    id: code-modernization
   239â†’    engine: openrewrite
   240â†’    recipes:
   241â†’      - org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.SimplifyBooleanExpression
   242â†’      - org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.UnnecessaryParentheses
   243â†’      - org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java11toJava17
   244â†’
   245â†’self_heal:
   246â†’  enabled: true
   247â†’  max_retries: 3
   248â†’```
   249â†’
   250â†’### Advanced Options
   251â†’
   252â†’**Specialized Execution Modes:**
   253â†’```bash
   254â†’# Execute only planner step (for debugging healing workflows)
   255â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --render-planner
   256â†’
   257â†’# Execute only LLM step with custom model
   258â†’TRANSFLOW_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06 \
   259â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --exec-llm-first
   260â†’
   261â†’# Execute OpenRewrite application step
   262â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --exec-orw-first
   263â†’
   264â†’# Apply first successful transformation and stop
   265â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --apply-first
   266â†’```
   267â†’
   268â†’**Testing and Development:**
   269â†’```bash
   270â†’# Test mode - uses mock implementations for all external services
   271â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --test-mode
   272â†’
   273â†’# Plan mode - shows execution plan without running
   274â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --plan
   275â†’
   276â†’# Reduce mode - processes healing results
   277â†’ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml --reduce
   278â†’```
   279â†’
   280â†’### Expected Workflow Output
   281â†’
   282â†’```
   283â†’Starting Transflow: java11-to-17-migration
   284â†’âś“ Cloning repository: https://gitlab.com/your-org/your-java-project.git
   285â†’âś“ Creating branch: workflow/java11-to-17-migration/20250905151234
   286â†’âś“ Executing recipe: org.openrewrite.java.migrate.Java11toJava17
   287â†’âś“ Committing changes: Apply Java 11 to 17 migration recipes
   288â†’âś“ Building project: tfw-java11-to-17-migration-20250905151234
   289â†’âś“ Pushing branch to remote
   290â†’âś“ Creating GitLab MR: https://gitlab.com/your-org/your-java-project/-/merge_requests/42
   291â†’
   292â†’Workflow completed successfully!
   293â†’  Branch: workflow/java11-to-17-migration/20250905151234
   294â†’  Build Version: 20250905151234-abc123
   295â†’  Duration: 2m 34s
   296â†’  MR URL: https://gitlab.com/your-org/your-java-project/-/merge_requests/42
   297â†’```
   298â†’
   299â†’### Error Handling and Self-Healing
   300â†’
   301â†’When builds fail and self-healing is enabled, the system will:
   302â†’
   303â†’1. **Analyze the failure** using LangGraph planner
   304â†’2. **Generate healing options** (human intervention, LLM fixes, additional recipes)
   305â†’3. **Execute options in parallel** with first-success-wins logic
   306â†’4. **Apply successful fix** and continue workflow
   307â†’5. **Create MR** with healing summary included
   308â†’
   309â†’**Self-Healing Output Example:**
   310â†’```
   311â†’âš  Build failed: compilation errors in Main.java:15
   312â†’đź”§ Self-healing enabled, starting recovery...
   313â†’âś“ Planner job completed: 3 healing options generated
   314â†’  â†’ Option 1: llm-exec (confidence: 0.8)
   315â†’  â†’ Option 2: org.openrewrite.java.cleanup.UnnecessaryParentheses
   316â†’  â†’ Option 3: human-step
   317â†’âś“ Executing healing options in parallel...
   318â†’âś“ llm-exec option succeeded after 45s
   319â†’âś“ Retrying build with healed changes...
   320â†’âś“ Build successful: tfw-java11-to-17-migration-20250905151234-healed
   321â†’âś“ Continuing workflow...
   322â†’```
   323â†’
   324â†’### Environment Configuration
   325â†’
   326â†’**Required Environment Variables:**
   327â†’- `GITLAB_TOKEN`: GitLab personal access token for MR creation
   328â†’- `GITLAB_URL`: GitLab instance URL (default: https://gitlab.com)
   329â†’
   330â†’**Optional Environment Variables:**
   331â†’- `TRANSFLOW_MODEL`: LLM model for healing (default: gpt-4o-mini@2024-08-06)
   332â†’- `TRANSFLOW_TOOLS`: MCP tools configuration JSON
   333â†’- `TRANSFLOW_LIMITS`: Execution limits configuration JSON
   334â†’- `NOMAD_ADDR`: Nomad cluster address for job submission
   335â†’
   336â†’### Integration with Existing Systems
   337â†’
   338â†’The transflow CLI integrates seamlessly with existing Ploy infrastructure:
   339â†’- **ARF Pipeline**: Reuses `ploy arf transform` for recipe execution
   340â†’- **Build System**: Uses existing `/v1/apps/:app/builds` API for validation
   341â†’- **Git Operations**: Leverages ARF git handling with automatic configuration
   342â†’- **Job Orchestration**: Uses existing Nomad job submission infrastructure
   343â†’
